guest_letters.title: Based website
guest_letters.body: Why aren’t we talking more about this IAC guy?? I just woke uop and checked bahut interesting site hai, there’s quotes and funny articles and board status?? We need more anons like iac to make cool shit. Im a very impressed pajeet. Writing this to see if it will get featured on the newspaper or not, im not sure how he set it up but if the !thing works then hello indus news! Im on da news niggaaaaaa
guest_letters.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of beef-eating controversy moved into the board today, sparking a torrent of debate on faith and culture. The system's center was marked by a scathing remark about sacred animals, which drew a deluge of responses.

A scattered high-pressure ridge of relationship queries formed later in the day, with one anon seeking advice on dating a UC girl with a Mallu Brahmin ex. This inquiry brought a light drizzle of comments on intercaste relationships and social compatibility.

Meanwhile, a cold front of caste hatred swept through the board, marked by calls for violence against low castes and scattered praise for a user named Dyaush.

A speculative heat wave of excitement arose over rumors of an upcoming girl group, with some posters expressing eagerness to see the group perform.

articles.title: Eating Beef Sparks Debate on Faith and Culture
articles.body:
Anon, going by the name 'Savarkar-pilled', sparked a heated debate by stating that having a sacred animal is 'retarded' and that eating beef is 'based'. They claimed that if people consider an animal a god, they should get their 'malnutritioned brain checked'.

Dyaush responded by arguing that the issue is not just about the animal itself, but also about the spiritual and moral layers that people associate with it.

They claimed that even if one doesn't care about animal welfare, the spiritual aspect is a separate concern.

Dyaush also discussed the concept of the Indus Cow, which is meant to be protected, and how some cows have mixed with other breeds and been sold.

They argued that just because some cows have been mistreated, it doesn't mean that the entire concept of protecting them is invalid. The conversation also touched on genetics, with Dyaush debunking the myth that the Y chromosome is weaker than the X chromosome.

They explained that the Y chromosome is fundamentally different from the X chromosome and that it's not accurate to say that it's weaker. Another topic that came up was dietary laws and their relationship to faith.

Dyaush argued that following dietary laws is more about the strength of one's faith than morality. They also discussed their own plans to recreate the Indus Valley Diet, which includes meat and seafood, as well as fermented foods.

The debate also got heated, with Dyaush accusing others of trying to 'shit the thread up' and expressing frustration with the direction the conversation was taking. They also made some inflammatory comments about other users, using derogatory language.

Throughout the thread, various topics were discussed, including faith, culture, genetics, and dietary laws. While some users expressed strong opinions on these topics, others seemed more open to discussion and exploration of different perspectives.

articles.title: Anon Seeks Advice on Dating a UC Girl with Mallu Brahmin Ex
articles.body:
Anon opened a discussion on dating a girl from a similar background, despite being a non-Brahmin upper caste individual. The girl shares common interests with Anon, but her ex-partner was a Mallu Brahmin, and she shows inconsistent interest.

Anon received various responses, with one suggesting that mixed individuals may further mix due to lacking a coherent identity. Another example was the Kayastha community, which originated from Savarna Shudra and eventually formed their distinct subculture.

Some responders shared their views on caste dynamics. One stated that upper castes who are traditional only marry within their Varna, while liberal upper castes are more open to marrying across castes and faiths.

A counterpoint was that lower castes also have preferences and won't marry outside their community. The conversation also touched on genetics and identity. A poster mentioned the AASI genetic drift and its implications.

Another respondent argued that individuals should prioritize marrying within their Varna to preserve their ethnic identity. The discussion also involved debates on eugenics and social hierarchy. One extreme view supported aggressive eugenics to create a 'supreme Aryan' caste.

However, most responders focused on personal choices and cultural nuances. Ultimately, Anon's situation remains uncertain, with advice ranging from exploring the girl's values and cultural background to being mindful of the complexities of inter-caste relationships.

articles.title: Anon Speculates on Upcoming Girl Group
articles.body:
Anon initiated a discussion on an upcoming girl group called 'gobarjeeta kumari'. The poster asked if anyone's sister was auditioning and expressed interest in seeing the group perform.

Another anon responded with enthusiasm, mentioning 'NEpyari supremacy' and expressing happiness that the group might not have a predominantly East Asian aesthetic.

This poster also shared a fond memory of a 'momo wali pyaari /nep/yaari didi' who sold momos with her mother.

The conversation took a different turn when an anon started fantasizing about secretly dating and having sex with one of the idols, calling it 'peak achivement' for any man.

Some anons responded with similar fantasies and objectifying comments about female idols. Anon also joked about the group's potential appearance, with one poster expressing hope that the group wouldn't feature someone as unattractive as a certain individual.

Some anons discussed the potential for the group to be a 'larp and bait' for certain individuals. The thread also touched on the topic of female idols and their perceived purity and singleness.

Anon discussed how idols have to appear pure and single to appeal to their audience, and one poster joked about wanting to see an idol perform in skimpy clothes.

The conversation concluded with anons continuing to speculate about the group's composition and potential success.

One poster expressed hope that the group would feature a variety of appearances, while another joked about the potential for 'gobarjeeta feminist organizations' to try to ban minors from becoming members.

articles.title: Anon Praises Dyaush, Others React
articles.body: Anon expressed appreciation for Dyaush, stating "I still appreciate dyaush for these kinos". Another anon asked Dyaush about his ability to claim multiple castes, saying "how do you become all the caste at same time". Anon also joked about being "OBCHAMAR,PONGA,MUGHALPUT,JHANT,GOOJAR,PADAV,CUCKTRI,CHAMAR". Dyaush responded, saying "You are welcome child, now you too can be me". The conversation also involved criticism of Dyaush as a "low caste sympathizer retard".

articles.title: Anon Calls for Violence Against Low Castes
articles.body: Anon opened the thread with a post calling for violence against low castes, stating 'Skin low castes alive!!' Anon responded with suggestions on how to approach hating low castes, advising to 'calm down' and 'not throw ideas that will make even us UC not want to associate with you.' Another anon argued that low castes are a drain on India, claiming they 'put false cases on innocent ppl' and 'steal seats in colleges and jobs from deserving ppl.' A respondent also mentioned specific cases of low-caste violence, including a case where a low-caste individual allegedly raped and murdered a woman.
